title: adenophora coelestis
intro: Adenophora coelestis, also known as the sweet-scented gilja or the skyflower, is a plant species native to East Asia and the Russian Far East. This perennial herb is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Adenophora coelestis include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its ability to improve respiratory health. Adenophora coelestis is commonly used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the dried roots or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
